pi_items.verified_value: 1 minute of Latitude is 1 nautical mile, but Longitude depends on the cosine of the latitude
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed: nautical mile is defined as 1 arc-minute of latitude. Longitude conversion verified: 1 degree longitude = 60*cos(latitude) nautical miles at any latitude

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating a Bridge Command maritime simulator scenario in the Solent environment (a real shipping area between Hampshire and Isle of Wight, UK). The scenario places a wreck at coordinates 50.7000°N, 1.3000°W and marks it with four IALA cardinal buoys (North, East, South, West) positioned 0.5 nautical miles from the wreck. All geographic coordinates, navigational calculations (haversine distance formula with Earth radius 3440.065 NM), bearing calculations, and maritime buoy standards (cardinal marks following IALA conventions) have been independently verified as accurate. The latitude/longitude conversion principle that 1 minute of latitude equals 1 nautical mile while longitude offsets depend on cos(latitude) is correct. The task uses synthetic data (requiring creation of new scenario files rather than importing existing data).
